question.text: What is the STT rate applicable to the seller of equity futures contracts?

answer.text: 0.02% of the traded price (applicable on the seller of futures)
0.10% of the traded price (applicable on both buyer and seller)
0.0125% of the premium (applicable on the buyer of futures)
0.05% of the settlement price (applicable on the clearing corporation)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** STT on equity futures: 0.02% on the seller, calculated on the actual traded price × quantity. For equity options: 0.10% on the seller when the option is exercised (on settlement price). These rates apply to exchange-traded equity derivatives only. **The Trap:** 0.10% is for options (on exercise), not futures. The buyer does NOT pay STT on futures — only the seller does. The rate is applied on traded price, not settlement price. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch9 covers STT rates. Series XIII compares STT applicability across segments (equity-only vs. currency/IR where no STT applies). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** STT impacts trading costs significantly for high-frequency equity F&O traders. Currency and IR derivatives have a cost advantage as they don't attract STT.
